content:
# Plan and Implement

## Change Summary

Added a bounded 30-second timeout to spawn_background_reap so that unkillable processes don't block the tokio task forever. After force_kill, the function now wraps child.wait() in tokio::time::timeout. If the timeout expires, a tracing::warn is logged and the task completes, dropping the Arc<ManagedChild> handle. Extracted a spawn_background_reap_with_timeout helper to allow the test to use a shorter timeout.

## Steps

0. [Completed] Read spawn_background_reap and surrounding code (ManagedChild, force_kill, wait, confirm_teardown, existing tests) to understand the current implementation
1. [Completed] Add BACKGROUND_REAP_TIMEOUT constant (30s) at line 32 alongside existing TEARDOWN_GRACE_PERIOD
2. [Completed] Refactor spawn_background_reap into a thin wrapper calling spawn_background_reap_with_timeout, which wraps child.wait() in tokio::time::timeout and logs a tracing::warn on expiry
3. [Completed] Add tokio::test background_reap_completes_on_timeout that spawns a long-lived sleep process, uses a 1ms timeout, and asserts the Arc is dropped after the task completes
4. [Completed] Verify new test passes: cargo test background_reap_completes_on_timeout
5. [Completed] Verify no clippy regressions in process_backend.rs (all 16 pre-existing clippy warnings are in other files)
6. [Completed] Verify cargo fmt --check passes
7. [Completed] Run full test suite: 756 passed, 0 failed

## Validation Evidence

- cargo test background_reap_completes_on_timeout -- --nocapture: 1 passed, 0 failed
- cargo test: 756 passed, 0 failed, 9 ignored (doc-tests)
- cargo clippy -- -D warnings | grep process_backend: no output (no new clippy warnings in modified file)
- cargo fmt --check: clean (no formatting issues)

## Outstanding Risks

- On platforms where SIGKILL is truly ineffective (e.g., process stuck in uninterruptible I/O 'D' state), the process will remain as a zombie after handle drop — this is inherent to the OS and the timeout correctly avoids blocking the tokio runtime in this case
- Pre-existing clippy warnings (16 in other files) remain unfixed — not introduced by this change
